prd.oneLiner: Connect content models, assets, authoring and review, approval, scheduled publishing, versioning and archive, and performance analysis.
prd.goal: Achieve 90% or more on-time publication of planned content and reduce approval waiting time.
prd.whyNow: Content drafts, image assets, approval feedback, and channel schedules are spread across documents and messages, making brand consistency and freshness hard to manage.
prd.problem: Content drafts, image assets, approval feedback, and channel schedules are spread across documents and messages, making brand consistency and freshness hard to manage.
prd.solution: Connect content models, assets, authoring and review, approval, scheduled publishing, versioning and archive, and performance analysis.
prd.alternatives: Email, chat, spreadsheets, and manual ledgers
prd.differentiator: Manage intake, processing, and operations data as one connected source of truth.

targets.name: Marketer or editor authoring, reviewing, and publishing content
targets.needs: Quickly register needed work and know its status.
targets.pain: Content drafts, image assets, approval feedback, and channel schedules are spread across documents and messages, making brand consistency and freshness hard to manage.

targets.name: Content operations and brand management coordinator
targets.needs: Manage priority and history and review operations metrics.
targets.pain: Content drafts, image assets, approval feedback, and channel schedules are spread across documents and messages, making brand consistency and freshness hard to manage.

targets.name: System and policy administrator
targets.needs: Manage access, policy, and audit history.
targets.pain: Content drafts, image assets, approval feedback, and channel schedules are spread across documents and messages, making brand consistency and freshness hard to manage.

scenarios.text: An operational user registers required work and checks its status.
An operator processes queued work and handles exceptions.
System and policy administrator reviews policy and access.
System and policy administrator reviews audit records and operating metrics.

prd.northStar: Share of operations requests completed on time

kpis.name: On-time processing rate
kpis.target: Achieve 90% or more on-time publication of planned content and reduce approval waiting time.
kpis.method: Monthly aggregation of state changes against target dates

kpis.name: Operations visibility
kpis.method: Share of records connected to reporting

inScope: Content draft, asset, and campaign-detail registration
Content, version, channel, approval, publication, and performance-status lookup
Authoring, review, approval, scheduled publishing, revision, and archive handling
Publishing schedule, content freshness, channel performance, and approval-bottleneck reporting
Content draft, asset, and campaign-detail registration validation and duplicate prevention
nonGoals: Replacing adjacent systems such as accounting or payroll
assumptions: User and organization data are available through company SSO.
Operators manage policy and classification criteria.
risks: Weak initial classification can cause incorrect processing.
Stale source data can reduce operational trust.
openQuestions: What defines urgent processing and approval?
What are the post-completion verification and reopen rules?
constraints: State, owner, and priority changes require audit logs.
Permission, policy, and state changes require audit logs.
